⚡ Simulate Real Interpreting Pressure
Interpreters correctly decide within a 2–3 second window. No backspace. No second take. This quiz scores accuracy and swiftness—miss it and you miss it.
5.5 seconds. That's all you get.
⚖ Legal Concepts mode: definition-matching, 30s per question, speed × accuracy scored.
🎤 Speech Interpreting
Hear a legal sentence. Interpret it aloud in the target language.
Your speech is transcribed and scored on accuracy, completeness, grammar, and fluency.
🔌 Tip: Recognition quality depends on your microphone, browser, and OS. For best results use Chrome on desktop with a clear mic in a quiet environment. If recognition is unreliable, use the text-input fallback.

Key & Legends

Usage Score ▼

Rates terms 1–10 by certification exam frequency and court usage.

  • 9–10 Critical — must know
  • 7–8 Important — high frequency
  • 5–6 Standard — comprehensive study
  • ≤4 Supplemental
Difficulty Score (IDI) ▼

Cross-linguistic difficulty (EN↔CN), semantic density, context dependence.

  • 9–10 Extremely difficult
  • 7–8 Challenging
  • 5–6 Moderate
  • ≤4 Straightforward
Quiz Scoring ▼

Per Question

Correct: swiftness = max(0, base − time). Incorrect: 0.

Quiz base = 3.5 (within 5.5s). Interpret base = 5.5 (within 11s).

Final Score (max ~150)

Accuracy (0–100) + Swiftness (0–50) + Fibonacci streak bonus (log-capped at 10.5).

Swiftness Sweet Spot

1.7–2.0s reaction = top-tier swiftness weight in Quiz mode. For Interpret mode the proportional window is wider.

10 Tiers

Bell-curve banding from Novice Observer to Grandmaster Interpreter.

Study Priority ▼
  • ★★★ High Usage + High Difficulty — study first
  • ★★ High Usage + Low Difficulty — quick wins
  • ★ Low Usage + High Difficulty — deepens skills
  • Reference: Low both — review as time permits
📝 Written Exam Practice
Practice for the NCSC written exam: 135 questions in 2h15m. You get 30 seconds per question. Bank: 215 questions across 5 categories (numbered for reference). Accuracy is what counts — speed is a small bonus.
⚖ Legal Concepts
325 bar-exam legal terms across 12 domains. Each question shows a legal term — pick the correct definition. No translations, no CJK — pure legal knowledge. 30 seconds per question.
